name: OLMES
tagline: Reproducible language-model evaluation with fixed prompts and standardized settings
metaDescription: OLMES is Ai2's open evaluation system for base and instruction-tuned LLMs. Fixed prompt formats and settings make benchmark results comparable across models.
overview: OLMES (Open Language Model Evaluation System) is the evaluation toolkit Ai2 (Allen Institute for AI) uses to score base and instruction-tuned language models across a range of tasks. It standardizes prompt formats, few-shot examples, and scoring settings so that results from different models can be compared on the same footing.

It is aimed at researchers and engineers who train or fine-tune LLMs and need benchmark numbers they can trust and reproduce. You point it at a Hugging Face model and one or more tasks, and it runs the evaluation and writes structured results to an output directory.

As a benchmark harness, OLMES builds on EleutherAI's lm-evaluation-harness with added support for task variants, instance-level prediction data (such as logprobs), custom metrics, and integration with external result storage. It ships ready-made task suites that reproduce the numbers reported in the OLMo, OLMo 2, OLMo 3, and TÜLU 3 papers.
features: Standardized OLMES task formats (curated few-shot examples, multiple-choice and cloze formulations) so scores compare fairly across models
Evaluates any Hugging Face model by path, plus a built-in model library for common references
Pre-built task suites that reproduce OLMo, OLMo 2, OLMo 3, and TÜLU 3 paper results
Records detailed instance-level data including logprobs, custom metrics, and metric aggregations
Optional vLLM backend via the gpu extra for faster inference
Helper flags like --inspect to preview a sample prompt and --dry-run to inspect the launch command

gettingStarted.intro: Clone the repo and install with uv or pip, then run an evaluation against a model and task.

steps.heading: Install
steps.body: Clone the repository and install with uv or pip. Add the gpu extra if you want vLLM support.
steps.code:
git clone https://github.com/allenai/olmes.git
cd olmes

# To install with uv:
uv sync
uv sync --group gpu # for vLLM support

# To install with pip:
pip install -e .
pip install -e ".[gpu]" # for vLLM support

steps.heading: Run an evaluation
steps.body: Point olmes at a model and a task, and set an output directory. This runs the standard OLMES version of ARC Challenge and stores results in the workspace folder.

steps.heading: Inspect or dry-run first
steps.body: Use --inspect to preview a sample prompt (with a small 5-instance eval), or --dry-run to print the launch command without running it. Run olmes --help for the full argument list.

steps.heading: Run a full task suite
steps.body: Pass one of the bundled suites to reproduce the tasks from a paper, such as the 10 multiple-choice tasks from the OLMES paper.

useCases: Benchmarking a newly trained or fine-tuned LLM on standardized tasks so the scores are comparable with other models
Reproducing the evaluation numbers reported in the OLMo, OLMo 2, OLMo 3, or TÜLU 3 papers
Collecting instance-level prediction data (such as logprobs) for deeper analysis of model behavior
Comparing multiple checkpoints or model variants under identical prompt formats and settings
